$schema_note: Plan Section 6.7 (Phase 4e: Terrain asset capture) placeholder-labeling record. Every asset listed here is a PLACEHOLDER, enhancement-only chrome-layer asset for the Knowledge Terrain graph mode. NONE of these assets is production-ready art, and none carries any production-readiness claim. Terrain mode is fully functional with zero of these assets present (see web/src/routes/graph/three/TerrainSurface.tsx, TerrainEnvironment.tsx, TerrainLandmarks.tsx for the graceful, non-throwing fallback wiring). The node/edge graph layer (O(1.5k-10k) elements) is untouched and remains fully procedural instanced-primitive per the plan's Section 3.2 rendering hard boundary -- nothing below is used anywhere near that layer.
tooling_substitution_note: The plan specifies 'Atelier MCP'. No engineering role in this session has Atelier MCP tools wired in. Atelier is a convenience wrapper around ComfyUI's own local HTTP API. Per this job's explicit instruction, generation instead used ComfyUI's native REST API directly (http://127.0.0.1:8188, /prompt + /history + /view + /upload/image) against the same local install at H:\LocalAI\ComfyUI (RTX 3080 Ti, 12GB VRAM) -- same models, same local GPU, no wrapper.

assets.model: FLUX.2-klein-4B (fp8_e4m3fn runtime-cast UNET weights via ComfyUI UNETLoader weight_dtype)
assets.prompt: seamless equirectangular 360 degree HDRI sky panorama, dusk gradient from deep indigo zenith to warm amber horizon glow, minimal soft clouds, smooth photographic sky dome, empty flat horizon line, no ground, no terrain, no text, no watermark, no logo, wide 2:1 panorama projection, calm atmospheric lighting
assets.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, signature, ground, terrain, buildings, people, animals, blurry, low quality, seams, visible tiling, harsh contrast, saturated colors
assets.known_limits: Not a true seamless-wrap panorama (a general-purpose text-to-image model, not a dedicated panorama tool) -- acceptable for a background HDRI at this enhancement-only tier, but a visible seam may be perceptible on close inspection.

assets.prompt: seamless equirectangular 360 degree HDRI sky panorama, cool blue violet twilight gradient, faint stars near zenith, soft horizon glow, smooth photographic sky dome, empty flat horizon line, no ground, no terrain, no text, no watermark, no logo, wide 2:1 panorama projection, calm atmospheric lighting
assets.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, signature, ground, terrain, buildings, people, animals, blurry, low quality, seams, visible tiling, harsh contrast, saturated colors
assets.known_limits: Same panorama-seam caveat as skybox-dusk.png. Not wired by default (skybox-dusk.png is the default in terrainAssetManifest.ts); available as an alternate skybox.

assets.prompt: grayscale matcap sphere reference chart, neutral gray clay material, soft even studio lighting, subtle topographic contour banding, smooth gradient highlight, no color, no hue, flat neutral gray backdrop, product render style, high detail
assets.negative_prompt: color, saturated, colorful, warm tones, cool tones, blue tint, orange tint, text, watermark, logo, background scene, busy background, blurry, low quality
assets.known_limits: A rendered sphere photo, not a studio-captured orthographic matcap sphere filling the entire frame (has visible background/edge margin) -- a reasonable placeholder approximation, not a technically ideal matcap capture. Wired as the default matcap in terrainAssetManifest.ts via THREE.MeshMatcapMaterial.

assets.prompt: grayscale matcap sphere reference chart, neutral stone material, soft diffused lighting, subtle specular highlight, topographic elevation contour shading, no color, no hue, flat neutral gray backdrop, product render style, high detail
assets.negative_prompt: color, saturated, colorful, warm tones, cool tones, blue tint, orange tint, text, watermark, logo, background scene, busy background, blurry, low quality
assets.known_limits: Same placeholder-sphere-photo caveat as matcap-clay.png. Not wired by default; available as an alternate matcap.

assets.model_mesh: Trellis2 (microsoft/TRELLIS.2-4B), image-conditioned single-mesh generation, mesh-only (no texture pass)
assets.model_reference_image: FLUX.2-klein-4B (fp8_e4m3fn), prompt: 'a small stylized fantasy stone obelisk landmark with carved rune markings, isolated single object centered on plain flat white background, video game 3d asset icon, clean readable silhouette, soft even studio lighting, no cast shadow gradient, orthographic-ish front three-quarter view'
assets.trellis2_pipeline: Trellis2LoadModel(microsoft/TRELLIS.2-4B, conv_backend=flex_gemm) -> LoadImage -> Trellis2PreProcessImage(remove_background=true) -> Trellis2MeshWithVoxelGenerator(pipeline_type=512) -> Trellis2Remesh -> Trellis2SimplifyMesh(target_face_num=4500) -> Trellis2FillHolesWithMeshlib -> Trellis2MeshWithVoxelToTrimesh -> Trellis2ExportMesh(glb)
assets.known_limits: Untextured/mesh-only (vertex-color or default material only, no baked texture pass) -- reduces pipeline complexity and generation time for a placeholder-tier asset; a textured pass (Trellis2MeshTexturing, as used by the ComfyUI-Trellis2 example 'LowPoly.json' workflow) is a documented available upgrade path, not attempted in this job.

assets.model_mesh: Trellis2 (microsoft/TRELLIS.2-4B), image-conditioned single-mesh generation, mesh-only (no texture pass)
assets.model_reference_image: FLUX.2-klein-4B (fp8_e4m3fn), prompt: 'a small stylized crystal spire landmark cluster with faceted geometric shards, isolated single object centered on plain flat white background, video game 3d asset icon, clean readable silhouette, soft even studio lighting, no cast shadow gradient, orthographic-ish front three-quarter view'
assets.trellis2_pipeline: identical pipeline to landmarks/obelisk.glb -- see that entry.

trellis2_environment_note: Trellis2 IS genuinely installed as a ComfyUI custom node (ComfyUI-Trellis2 in H:\LocalAI\ComfyUI\custom_nodes) with its main model weights present locally. Two concrete environment gaps were found and remediated as local, non-network, non-'new dependency' fixes, documented transparently here: (1) the Trellis2LoadModel node additionally required a Facebook Dinov3 vision-encoder checkpoint (facebook/dinov3-vitl16-pretrain-lvd1689m) that was present on disk, but only inside a separate, parallel ComfyUI install at H:\LocalAI\ComfyUI_Trellis2 (not the main H:\LocalAI\ComfyUI install used here) -- fixed by creating an NTFS directory junction at H:\LocalAI\ComfyUI\models\facebook\dinov3-vitl16-pretrain-lvd1689m pointing at that already-downloaded folder (zero new download, zero duplication, read-only reference). (2) The default 'spconv' sparse-convolution backend module was not installed in the main ComfyUI's Python venv; rather than installing a new package, the run instead used the 'flex_gemm' conv_backend option, which the ComfyUI-Trellis2 custom node already ships as a prebuilt wheel for this exact Python/Torch combination (wheels/Windows/Torch280/flex_gemm-...cp312-win_amd64.whl) and which was already importable in the venv -- no new package installation was performed.
trellis2_landmark_count_note: The plan caps landmark GLBs at 'at most 6'. Two were generated (obelisk, spire) to prove genuine end-to-end feasibility within this job's effort budget; the same ComfyUI prompt/pipeline in this manifest can be re-run with a different reference image to produce up to 4 more within the plan's cap, if desired in a future job.
